gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/57)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/57)
text: مَنْ زَارَ أَخَاهُ فِي اللَّهِ وَ لِلَّهِ جَاءَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يَخْطُرُ بَيْنَ قَبَاطِيَّ مِنْ نُورٍ وَ لَا يَمُرُّ بِشَيْ‏ءٍ إِلَّا أَضَاءَ لَهُ حَتَّى يَقِفَ بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ مَرْحَباً وَ إِذَا قَالَ مَرْحَباً أَجْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ الْعَطِيَّةَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ali Bin Al Nahdy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who visits his brother regarding Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and for Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, would come on the Day of Judgement swaying between gowns of light, and not passing by anything except shining upon it, until he pauses in front of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic. So Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would be Saying to him: “Welcome!” And when He<sup>azwj</sup> Says: “Welcome!” Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would be Liberal towards him with the Gifts’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Ali ibn al-Nahdi from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “If one visits his brother (in belief) due to Allah’s relation and for the sake of Allah, on the Day of Judgment he will come, walking elegantly in a Coptic gown of light and will not pass by anything but that it will shine until he will come before Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, and Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, will say, ‘Welcome.’ When He will say welcome. He, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, will make his reward very handsome.’”
